title: Kiln Law — .kiln/law.json, the locked acceptance-check index
description: The test-lock spine (BLUEPRINT §5/§5.1). Asimov writes it pre-lock (lock_commit null, every sha256 map empty); kiln-law index locks it (lock_commit = HEAD at index time, every check's sha256 map covering exactly its files); immutable after. The §5 sequence is index FIRST, then one 'test(law): lock acceptance gates' commit carrying the gates + this file. Cross-field invariants a JSON schema cannot express — duplicate check ids rejected, sha256 maps EMPTY pre-lock and covering EXACTLY files when locked — are enforced by validateLaw in src/law.mjs, which mirrors this schema field-for-field and runs on every kiln-law command (no ajv, no deps). Tamper model: kiln-law verify — and kiln-law run, BEFORE executing any check — re-hashes every locked path against sha256 AND git-diffs it against the commit that locked it (lock_commit for paths already in it; the first descendant commit that introduced the gates otherwise); ANY mismatch exits 2 with a machine-readable 'TAMPER: <path>' line per offender.

lock_commit.description: HEAD at index time — the last PRE-gate commit (git content-addressing: law.json cannot carry the sha of the commit that contains it); the lock commit that follows index is its first descendant touching the locked paths, and the tamper gate's git arm anchors there; null before indexing

checks.description: Exactly ONE entry per SC — coverage is arithmetic, not judgment

id.description: The SC id this check executes (globally unique across milestones)

milestone.description: The master-plan milestone the SC belongs to (e.g. M2)

kind.description: Check class; 'probe' entries are browser-probe TEMPLATES — wired but inert until a later phase makes them runnable (the runner skips them with PROBE_DEFERRED)

cmd.description: The exact command the runner executes with cwd=projectPath; may be empty ONLY for kind 'probe' templates

files.description: This check's locked file paths, projectPath-relative (no leading '/', no '..') — they are sha256 keys and git pathspecs

sha256.description: path → sha256 of the locked content; EMPTY pre-lock, covers exactly files when locked (written only by kiln-law index)

expected.description: Pass condition — exit code IS the verdict, no prose parsing

timeout_s.description: Per-check execution deadline in seconds (the runner SIGKILLs past it)

pre_satisfied.description: §5.1 brownfield lifecycle: GREEN at lock time — excluded from flip accounting, guarded as a regression instead
